issue_title: trunk does not respect 64-bit mpfr exponent range
issue_body: MPFR uses the same default exponent range on 32 and 64-bit platforms. It is possible to use an extended 64-bit exponent range on platforms where long is 64 bits. The current trunk does not support it.

issue_title: Document exceptions raised by Kernel.run
issue_body:
It seems that curio.run() may raise a TaskError. This is currently not documented, the documentation for Kernel.run does not mention any exceptions, and the documentation for TaskError states that this is only raised by a taks's join() method.

It would be nice to document the current behavior.

It would be even nicer (in my opinion) to also just re-raise the original exception. I don't see much point in wrapping it in a TaskError. In particular, when unit-testing a program that uses curio curoutines, this makes working with the tests rather awkward.

issue_title: Why doesn't "spawn" return right away?
issue_body:
Documentation says: "Spawn does not return to the caller until the
new task has been scheduled and executed for at least one cycle."

It means spawn is holding the caller on the line and this is some kind
of a sync behaviour. (At least for the initial steps.) I think caller task
should not be bothered with the initial steps of the spawned tasks.
Is this a technical necessity or any other thing?

Suppose a parent task is spawning/off-loading to many child tasks.
In this case parent's throughput is reduced by such sync behaviour.

issue_title: Missing Spy API functions
issue_body:
Here are some Spy API functions that are still missing (`withArgs` seems like it might be tough):

```python
spy.thisValues # Array of this objects, spy.thisValues[0] is the this object for the first call.

spy.withArgs()
# Creates a spy that only records calls when the received arguments match those passed
# to withArgs. This is useful to be more expressive in your assertions, where you can access
# the spy with the same call.

spy.calledOn(obj);
# Returns true if the spy was called at least once with obj as this. calledOn also accepts
# a matcher spyCall.calledOn(sinon.match(fn))

spy.alwaysCalledOn(obj); # Returns true if the spy was always called with obj as this.

spy.printf("format string", [arg1, arg2, ...])
# Returns the passed format string with the following replacements performed:
# %n
#     the name of the spy "spy" by default)
# %c
#     the number of times the spy was called, in words ("once", "twice", etc.)
# %C
#     a list of string representations of the calls to the spy, with each call prefixed by a newline and four
#     spaces
# %t
#     a comma-delimited list of this values the spy was called on
# %n
#     the formatted value of the nth argument passed to printf
# %*
#     a comma-delimited list of the (non-format string) arguments passed to printf 
```

issue_title: HAND
issue_body:
For HAND, 

a. Send HID object, numpy buffer (of `mp.Array()` origin), and a shared flag (i.e. poison pill for the remote process)
    - `dev.stop()` sets the shared flag to `True`, ending the remote process
    - `dev.start()` resets the shared flag, dumps 40 recent values from the buffer, fills the numpy array with `np.nan`s
    - `dev.read()` returns a copy of the numpy buffer, and clears it
    - `dev.clear()` sets all vals in buffer to `np.nan`
    - `dev.write()` WILL send bytes to HID (use Queue?). Implement once we have the other side ready...
    - `dev.close()` calls `_hid.close()`

issue_title: Binary messages between kernel and browser
issue_body: Does IPython support binary messages between kernel and browser? Modern browsers can receive binary and save it in an ArrayBuffer. This would make sending coordinates and topology data to the browser more efficient. It is often in an binary format in the kernel and can consumed as such in the browser thus avoiding converting/parsing to/from JSON.

issue_title: Fetching BibTeX meta is not clear in the doc
issue_body:
Hi,

According to the doc, I can think of two ways to get a BibTeX metadata for a given ISBN:

* Either `meta(isbn)` and formatting it.
* Either `doi2tex(doi(isbn))`.

The second method seems to be more reliable, in particular as it relies on the DOI system, which is using a unique identifier and gives better results than `meta` in my experience.

However, taken from [https://www.doi.org/factsheets/ISBN-A.html](the ISBN-A page on doi.org), they say:

> ISBN-As do not automatically exist for every ISBN; they exist only once the agency has registered them in the DOI System.

Whereas when looking at [your code](https://github.com/xlcnd/isbnlib/blob/master/isbnlib/_ext.py#L47) you always derive an ISBN-A from a given ISBN13.

For my use case, I would need a way to check whether the generated ISBN-A is indeed a valid ISBN-A linked to any DOI or not. And reading the doc and looking a bit at the code, I was not sure what is the expected behavior of `doi2tex(doi(isbn))` when the ISBN-A does not exist.

Thanks a lot!

P.S.: As I do not have examples of ISBNs without associated ISBN-A, I cannot check this easily =( Sorry if this is already documented somewhere else.

issue_title: ad_remove_members_from_groups with fix=True does not handle case differences in attribute keys
issue_body:
Members can silently fail being removed from a group if there are case differences in attribute keys
```
# ldap3/extend/microsoft/removeMembersFromGroups.py
def ad_remove_members_from_groups(...):
    ...
    member_to_remove = [member for member in members_dn if member in existing_member]
```
Where
```
existing_member = ['CN=bar,CN=Users,DC=foo,DC=local'] 
```
and
```
members_dn = ['cn=bar,cn=Users,dc=foo,dc=local'] 
```
(or vice versa).

issue_title: xvfb-run failed to start
issue_body:
After using this package, I have run into an issue with the invocation of xvfb-run under the hood. Each virtual headless server xvfb-run spins up will have spin down time. My script calls imgkit synchronously with repetitious invocations of the xvfb-run. The default server it tries is always the same (:99) of which each new one tries this server and fails as the process is still open in memory. A sleep of 100MS has been the work around.

The solution would be to tell xvfb-run with the option `-a, --auto-servernum Try to get a free server number, starting at 99, or the argument to --server-num.` 

Is there a way currently to pass this option down from imgkit to the xvfb-run process being called?

If not, I will submit a PR of this possibility.

issue_title: CPE 2.3 FS parsing error
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm dealing with an error when parsing an FS taken from the official NVD CPE dictionary:
http://static.nvd.nist.gov/feeds/xml/cpe/dictionary/official-cpe-dictionary_v2.3.xml

It seems like there is an issue with the regular expression(s) used there: 
https://github.com/nilp0inter/cpe/blob/develop/cpe/cpe2_3_fs.py#L85

The error occurs when parsing FS. If the FS contains an escaped colon ( \: ), the regex fails to grab each part correctly. It seems that the pattern [^\:]+ doesn't behave as intended.

How to reproduce:
```python
>>> from cpe import CPE
>>> c = CPE('cpe:2.3:a:canonical:update-manager:1\:0.134.11.1:*:*:*:*:*:*:*')
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/cpe/cpe.py", line 311, in __new__
    raise NotImplementedError(errmsg)
NotImplementedError: Version of CPE not implemented
>>>
```

Parsing CPE 2.2 names works like a charm and colon is escaped as it should:
```python
>>> c = CPE('cpe:/a:canonical:update-manager:1%3a0.134.11.1')
>>> print c.as_fs()
cpe:2.3:a:canonical:update-manager:1\:0.134.11.1:*:*:*:*:*:*:*
```

However, when taking it back to a 2.3 name:
```python
>>> CPE(c.as_fs())
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/cpe/cpe.py", line 311, in __new__
    raise NotImplementedError(errmsg)
NotImplementedError: Version of CPE not implemented
>>> 
```

If you have any lead on how to fix this by just modifying the regex it would be cool :). Otherwise it means partially refactoring FS parser, well, I guess.

Thanks !

issue_title: Issue with SCEUA/MPI
issue_body:
Dear spotpy team,

I've encountered an issue with the MPI implementation in SpotPy using the SCEUA algorithm. As you can see in the attached files, the value of the objective function seems not to be handled correctly after the burn in phase. When the complex evolution starts in parallel mode, the value of the objective function isn't updated anymore in the status function but correctly written to the database. This leads to a situation, where the algorithm can't work correctly and acts as some kind of mc algorithm. However, the serial version works completely as it is intended.

I've attached a few files to  illustrate the issue. There is a rosenbrock optimization problem just like you offer in the spotpy sample folder. It's calculated twice, once in serial mode and once in mpi mode. As you can see in the out file, in the mpi version, the value of the objective function in the status function isn't updated any more after the burn in phase (nopt=2, ngs=24, npt=120). Anyway, the correct value is written to the csv file. Please also note the x_y_trace files. The serial version reaches convergence after about 7200 trials, the mpi version converges after 77000 trials. I even run some test cases where convergence was never reached in mpi mode.

Kind regards

Patrick

[sceua_mpi.zip](https://github.com/thouska/spotpy/files/136758/sceua_mpi.zip)

issue_title: figure out how to use config() in a DRY way without screwing up config override
issue_body:
Say you have an app where you're doing a lot of things based on some `DEBUG` variable. You have a couple of options for implementation:
1. you could cache the value of `config('DEBUG', parser=bool)`, but that potentially screws up using config override in the tests
2. you could write `config('DEBUG', parser=bool)` over and over again, but that's awful for a variety of reasons

@ErikRose dislikes defining the types at call time, too, for similar reasons to the above.

This issue covers figuring out a better way to do this.

issue_title: namecheap authentication fails for permissioned domain management
issue_body:
The following lines check to see if the domain name is returned by Namecheap's API

https://github.com/AnalogJ/lexicon/blob/master/lexicon/providers/namecheap.py#L54-L63

Namecheap's API seems to be halfassed and this won't work as expected.

please bear with me on this, it's going to be odd to explain.

The only domains that seem to appear in this list are ones which are OWNED by the authenticating account. The list does not include items that are MANAGED by the authenticating account.  Namecheap's backend provisioning systems use an ACL (Access Control List) that allows for an domain "Owner" to proxy/delegate different responsibilities for a domain to other users of their system.

In my case, "Account1" is the `owner` of all domains, while "Account2" has been delegated admin privileges for **only** the DNS settings of a subset of domains.  This was done to allow a tier of company staff to manage DNS via the control panel AND also enable API access -- but keeping the staff and API away from registration/transfer/etc operations.

Privileged domains don't seem to appear on this paginated view and will trigger lexicon exiting.

A potential check would be the `namecheap.domains.getinfo` endpoint (https://www.namecheap.com/support/api/methods/domains/get-info.aspx)

Supplying a `&DomainName=example.com` argument will result in a valid response if the user has permissions:

```
<ApiResponse Status="OK" xmlns="http://api.namecheap.com/xml.response">
  <Errors />
  <Warnings />
  <RequestedCommand>namecheap.domains.getinfo</RequestedCommand>
  <CommandResponse Type="namecheap.domains.getInfo">
    <DomainGetInfoResult Status="Ok" ID="11111111111" DomainName="EXAMPLE.COM" OwnerName="USERNAME" IsOwner="false" IsPremium="false">
      <DomainDetails>
        <CreatedDate>02/15/2000</CreatedDate>
        <ExpiredDate>02/15/3000</ExpiredDate>
        <NumYears>0</NumYears>
      </DomainDetails>
      <LockDetails />
      <Whoisguard Enabled="False">
        <ID>0</ID>
      </Whoisguard>
      <PremiumDnsSubscription>
        <UseAutoRenew>false</UseAutoRenew>
        <SubscriptionId>-1</SubscriptionId>
        <CreatedDate>0001-01-01T00:00:00</CreatedDate>
        <ExpirationDate>0001-01-01T00:00:00</ExpirationDate>
        <IsActive>false</IsActive>
      </PremiumDnsSubscription>
      <DnsDetails ProviderType="FREE" IsUsingOurDNS="true" HostCount="5" EmailType="No Email Service" DynamicDNSStatus="false" IsFailover="false">
        <Nameserver>dns1.registrar-servers.com</Nameserver>
        <Nameserver>dns2.registrar-servers.com</Nameserver>
      </DnsDetails>
      <Modificationrights All="false">
        <Rights Type="hosts">OK</Rights>
      </Modificationrights>
    </DomainGetInfoResult>
  </CommandResponse>
  <Server>PHX01APIEXT03</Server>
  <GMTTimeDifference>--5:00</GMTTimeDifference>
  <ExecutionTime>0.384</ExecutionTime>
</ApiResponse>
```

Note this section which correlates to the ACL for `hosts` being enabled:

```
<Modificationrights All="false">
        <Rights Type="hosts">OK</Rights>
</Modificationrights>
```

If a user requests this endpoint on a domain they are not permissioned for, it's an error:

```
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<ApiResponse Status="ERROR" xmlns="http://api.namecheap.com/xml.response">
  <Errors>
    <Error Number="4011103">Access Denied</Error>
  </Errors>
  <Warnings />
  <RequestedCommand>namecheap.domains.getinfo</RequestedCommand>
  <CommandResponse Type="namecheap.domains.getInfo">
    <DomainGetInfoResult ID="0" IsOwner="false" IsPremium="false">
      <DomainDetails>
        <NumYears>0</NumYears>
      </DomainDetails>
      <LockDetails />
      <Whoisguard>
        <ID>0</ID>
      </Whoisguard>
      <PremiumDnsSubscription>
        <UseAutoRenew>false</UseAutoRenew>
        <SubscriptionId>-1</SubscriptionId>
        <CreatedDate>0001-01-01T00:00:00</CreatedDate>
        <ExpirationDate>0001-01-01T00:00:00</ExpirationDate>
        <IsActive>false</IsActive>
      </PremiumDnsSubscription>
      <DnsDetails IsUsingOurDNS="false" HostCount="0" DynamicDNSStatus="false" IsFailover="false" />
      <Modificationrights />
    </DomainGetInfoResult>
  </CommandResponse>
  <Server>PHX01APIEXT02</Server>
  <GMTTimeDifference>--5:00</GMTTimeDifference>
  <ExecutionTime>0.114</ExecutionTime>
</ApiResponse>
```

i don't know enough of their system to understand all the edge cases of what valid dns rights would look like in a variety of situations, but just doing a simple error check made this library usable for me.

issue_title: Issue with HP Comware with banners that require Enter to accept
issue_body:
Hello,

I noticed an issue with version 1.3 and 1.4 of Netmiko that was not a problem with version 1.2.8 of the package.

My HP comware switches have a disclaimer that requires the user to press the enter or y key to accept.  When setting up a ConnectHandler object, the process hangs.  This does not happen on my HP switches that do not have a disclaimer.   I have traced the hanging to the session_preparation() function in the hp_comware_ssh.py file.  Between versions 1.2.8 and 1.3 the function was changed from:

def session_preparation(self):
        """
        Prepare the session after the connection has been established.
        Extra time to read HP banners.
        """
        delay_factor = self.select_delay_factor(delay_factor=0)
        time.sleep(2 * delay_factor)
        self.clear_buffer()
        self.set_base_prompt()
        self.disable_paging(command="\nscreen-length disable\n")

to

def session_preparation(self):
        """
        Prepare the session after the connection has been established.
        Extra time to read HP banners.
        """
        self._test_channel_read(pattern=r'[>\]]')
        self.set_base_prompt()
        self.disable_paging(command="\nscreen-length disable\n")

Thanks,
Bob

issue_title: Embedded AP support
issue_body:
Would be nice to have an extension to ConnectionHandler that added 'enterAP' and 'exitAP' methods. The commands included below work if simply processed from top to bottom (ie take away the functions - I just included them to illustrate a class extension). Important to clear any existing sessions to get get a predictable prompt. Also important to have an extra carriage return after entering password. 

from netmiko import ConnectHandler
router_ssh = {
        'device_type': 'cisco_ios',
        'ip': "10.5.2.1",
        'username': "adm",
        'password': "pwd",
        'port' : 22, 
        'secret': 'ENABLE', 
        'verbose': True, 
        }

##extensions to ConnectHandler###
def enterAP(connection, user, pwd):
    connection.remote_conn.sendall('service-module wlan-ap 0 session clear\n')
    connection.remote_conn.sendall('\n')
    connection.read_until_pattern('#')
    connection.remote_conn.sendall('service-module wlan-ap 0 session\n')
    connection.read_until_pattern('sername')
    connection.remote_conn.sendall(user+'\n')
    connection.read_until_pattern('ssword')
    connection.remote_conn.sendall(pwd+'\n')
    connection.remote_conn.sendall('\n')

def exitAP(connection):
    connection.remote_conn.sendall(chr(30))
    connection.remote_conn.sendall('x')
    connection.remote_conn.sendall('disconnect')
    connection.remote_conn.sendall('\n')
##extensions to ConnectHandler###


ssh_connect = ConnectHandler(**router_ssh)
ssh_connect.enterAP()
print ssh_connect.find_prompt()
output = ssh_connect.send_command('show ip int br')
print output
ssh_connect.exitAP()
print ssh_connect.find_prompt()

So turns out it's dash-fast, as in "lacks too many useful features" (probably because it's still in alpha). The `printf "%b" "\033]11;#ff0000\007"` does nothing so I guess you should add it to the list. I tried to make it work with wal by having a `config.yml` that contains non-color options and an auto-generated `colors.yml` that has colors (used [termcolors](https://github.com/stayradiated/termcolors) to convert to Alacritty's format), what I did was add this function:

```
function fix_alacritty() {
	local dir="${HOME}/.config/alacritty"
	rm "${dir}/alacritty.yml"
	cat "${dir}/config.yml" "${dir}/colors.yml" >> "${dir}/alacritty.yml"
}

```
And call it in the script that gets executed after wal, turns out Alacritty needs everything to be in specific positions for the thing to work (it is possible with some mad regex-foo or yaml parsing, both of which I'm no good at and not going to try doing since the benefits aren't that good, not being able to change colors on the fly is a bummer)

So I guess it's not really an issue with wal but I'm just letting you know

issue_title: Support __repr__ on nodes
issue_body:
When creating parts of an AST from scratch, figuring out the actual types to use (as well as their correct constructor parameters), is currently a manual (and error-prone), process:
1. Create a source close to the intended code
2. Dump the AST, using `FileAST.show()`
3. Guess what types are needed, and refine until the correct structure is achieved.

Implementing [`__repr__`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html#repr) would definitely help, as step 3 would be a copy/paste of the `__repr__` output.

For example, using `tests_list_children`'s structure, `repr(comp)` could return:
```
Compound(block_items=[BinaryOp(op='+',
                               left=Constant(type='float',
                                             value='5.6'
                                             ),
                               right=Constant(type='char',
                                              value='t'
                                              )
                               ),
                      BinaryOp(op='-',
                               left=BinaryOp(op='+',
                                             left=Constant(type='float',
                                                           value='5.6'
                                                           ),
                                             right=Constant(type='char',
                                                            value='t'
                                                            )
                                             ),
                               right=Constant(type='char',
                                              value='t'
                                              )
                               ),
                      Constant(type='float',
                               value='5.6'
                               ),
                      Constant(type='char',
                               value='t'
                               )
                     ]
         )
```

issue_title: Feature request: Add IPython code completion
issue_body:
Currently, `win32com` code completion on COM objects only works for methods, and not properties, and the present feature requests concerns ensuring that properties are supported as well.

Given that the PythonWin shell already supports code completion, I can see how this could be considered outside the scope of the project, but given the popularity of Jupyter/IPython notebooks as a means of writing exploratory Python code, and given that IPython comes with an API for adding third-party code completion logic, I figured it would be worth checking whether or not this would be of interest. Certainly it would be very helpful for myself and those of my colleagues that deal with Microsoft automation on a daily basis.

The code completion in IPython 6.2.1 is handled by `core.completer.IPCompleter`, and the API for adding custom code completers is provided by [`IPython.utils.generics.complete_object`](https://github.com/ipython/ipython/blob/fc7d8a365a33f0de01b1b19b8a2a8ecd42f61f83/IPython/utils/generics.py#L18). One gotcha is that with `complete_object` being based on [`simplegeneric`](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/simplegeneric), only one completer may be provided for any given type. Luckily, all types generated by `makepy` will inherit from `win32com.client.DispatchBaseClass`, and adding code completion boils down to running

    from IPython.utils.generics import complete_object
    import win32com.client

    @complete_object.when_type(win32com.client.DispatchBaseClass)
    def complete_dispatch_base_class(obj, prev_completions):
        try:
            ole_props = set(obj._prop_map_get_).union(set(obj._prop_map_put_))
            return list(ole_props) + prev_completions
        except AttributeError:
            pass

This works, and at some point was the approach taken by e.g. [pandas](https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/) (after a simple check that `'IPython' in sys.modules`); in the case of pandas, [they switched](https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/pull/5050/commits/c7f6560b4aebb3431e4f203d302a6a5231c7d364) to the more elegant approach of simply defining `__dir__`, which is also [what IPython suggests](https://ipython.org/ipython-doc/dev/config/integrating.html#tab-completion).

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6169306/35823047-83e5530a-0aae-11e8-81d5-f46a6989442e.png)
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6169306/35823055-885e0e22-0aae-11e8-83d8-a7c1d51ba81e.png)

issue_title: Separate Client Initiated Renego and Secure Renego Checks
issue_body:
When testing session negotiation with Netty (Java TCP I/O framework) the check for client initiated session renegotiation leads to an error because of how Netty handles 'unsupported client renegotiation'. issue_title: STANDARD_P2P_MESSAGES does not support BIP130
issue_body:
Hi, 
protocol versions >= 70012 implement BIP130. 

In BIP130 remote nodes send a 'sendheaders' message to discover if they can send headers with dedicated messages, and not as inv items.

The current STANDARD_P2P_MESSAGES does not wrap the sendheaders message, causing pycoinnet to crash with protocol versions > 70011.

issue_title: Speed up continuous integration
issue_body:
This issue discusses ways we can speed up continuous integration by being a bit selective about what we include. This seems like it would particularly useful for @santoshphilip while developing the `py90dot1` project, as well as for any other contributors. Continuous integration is great, but when it's slow it becomes a lot less useful as immediate feedback.

My suggestions:

1. Remove the OSX builds from continuous integration entirely

They can take a couple of hours to run on Travis, while the Linux builds there are done in under five minutes. Windows on Appveyor comes in just under 30 minutes. Since @santoshphilip and I both develop on OSX, this is also the least likely place for us to miss a platform-specific issue.

2. Only test the most recent release version of Python 2 and Python 3, and of EnergyPlus

We currently cover 2.7, 3.5 and 3.6 for EnergyPlus versions 8.6, 8.7 and 8.8 (just 8.7 and 8.8 on Appveyor). Removing specific support for Python 3.5, and EnergyPlus 8.5 and 8.6 would reduce the Appveyor testing time in particular since jobs run one after another on that platform. By only testing the most recent versions, we could expect to get the time down from 30 minutes to around 10.

3. Run comprehensive tests for releases

We can make a way to only run OSX builds and builds for older versions of Python and EnergyPlus when merging into `master`. Then the only time we'd need to wait for hours on CI would be when preparing to merge `develop` into `master` for a release, or for any hotfixes that may need to go straight to `master`.

issue_title: Publishing linux egg files is problematic
issue_body:
Since version 1.1.8 a generic linux binary egg has been distributed through pypi. I'm guessing this is to help users that won't or can't install a compiler. Unfortunately this causes problems for users of easy-install that don't have the same "python 2.7" used to build the egg.

For example, when installing pykerberos for pypy easy_install grabs the egg file:

$ ./bin/pypy -m easy_install pykerberos
Searching for pykerberos
Reading https://pypi.python.org/simple/pykerberos/
Best match: pykerberos 1.1.9
Downloading https://pypi.python.org/packages/2.7/p/pykerberos/pykerberos-1.1.9-py2.7-linux-x86_64.egg#md5=4e6672337f0420c6964de0319566decf
Processing pykerberos-1.1.9-py2.7-linux-x86_64.egg
...

The installation completes without issue, but the interpreter faults when importing kerberos:

./bin/pypy
Python 2.7.10 (f3ad1e1e1d6215e20d34bb65ab85ff9188c9f559, Sep 01 2015, 11:00:11)
[PyPy 2.6.1 with GCC 4.9.2] on linux2
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>>> import kerberos
Fatal Python error: PyThreadState_Get: no current thread
Aborted

Reinstalling with pip avoids the problem since pip does not support egg packages and builds the project from source:

./bin/pypy -m pip install pykerberos
Collecting pykerberos
  Downloading pykerberos-1.1.9.tar.gz
Building wheels for collected packages: pykerberos
  Running setup.py bdist_wheel for pykerberos
...

$ ./bin/pypy 
Python 2.7.10 (f3ad1e1e1d6215e20d34bb65ab85ff9188c9f559, Sep 01 2015, 11:00:11)
[PyPy 2.6.1 with GCC 4.9.2] on linux2
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>>> import kerberos

There is nothing you can do to solve this problem in pykerberos. I would suggest removing the egg packages from pypi to avoid problems for future users. Also, note that pypi disallows uploading generic wheel binaries to avoid this exact problem. See the note here:

https://python-packaging-user-guide.readthedocs.org/en/latest/distributing/#platform-wheels

issue_title: locate* functions fail in Python 3.7
issue_body:
In python 3.7, the locateCenterOnScreen function returns the following errors when the image is located (does NOT fail if there is no matching image). I've run the same code (on Windows 10) with Python 3.6 without error.

The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "C:/Py/test37/test37.py", line 4, in <module>
    loc = bot.locateCenterOnScreen('chrome_icon.png')
  File "C:\Py\test37\venv37\lib\site-packages\pyscreeze\__init__.py", line 295, in locateCenterOnScreen
    coords = locateOnScreen(image, **kwargs)
  File "C:\Py\test37\venv37\lib\site-packages\pyscreeze\__init__.py", line 266, in locateOnScreen
    retVal = locate(image, screenshotIm, **kwargs)
  File "C:\Py\test37\venv37\lib\site-packages\pyscreeze\__init__.py", line 250, in locate
    points = tuple(locateAll(needleImage, haystackImage, **kwargs))
RuntimeError: generator raised StopIteration

Per the Python 3.7 release notes ([https://docs.python.org/3/whatsnew/3.7.html](https://docs.python.org/3/whatsnew/3.7.html)), it appears this issue derives from the following change:
Due to an oversight, earlier Python versions erroneously accepted the following syntax:
f(1 for x in [1],)

class C(1 for x in [1]):
    pass
Python 3.7 now correctly raises a SyntaxError, as a generator expression always needs to be directly inside a set of parentheses and cannot have a comma on either side, and the duplication of the parentheses can be omitted only on calls. (Contributed by Serhiy Storchaka in bpo-32012 and bpo-32023.)

issue_title: Include credit trading fees
issue_body:
Hi @mementum,

When short stock, ie. credit trading, I will be charged by my broker.
The fees is calculated in this way:
If I short stock in Jun 1, buy back at Aug 1, and the credit interest is 10% per year.
For each day between Jun 1 and Aug 1, I have to pay:  stock price * volume * 10% / 365 in **cash**.

When do a pair trading, this part of cost is crucial to find out whether a trade is profitable.

import pip

issue_title: 雪球组合同一时间发出2个及以上的调仓指令时，银河客户端下单出现BUG
issue_body:
使用银河客户端跟踪雪球组合时，跟踪的雪球组合在同一时间点发出2个及2个以上的调仓指令时，easytrader不能正确下达指令。
如：
同一时间发出股票A由15%→0%，股票B由0→15%，easytrader不能下达买入15%股票B的指令给银河客户端，且从easytrader反馈信息来看，将股票A和股票B都执行为卖出动作。

同理，当雪球组合在同一时间发出3个或更多的调仓指令时，买入卖出方向相同或不同时，都会触发此BUG，望大神解决。
（LOG截图已私信发送）

issue_title: Occasionally painfully slow performance over a network
issue_body:
I generally run es2csv from my local machine against a remote EC2 Linux instance over a VPN connection. Sometimes I get 600-800 docs/s and sometimes I get 10-20 docs/s. Everything that I control is the same. CPU utilization on the remote machine is very low. The same query run an hour later may run at 10x the speed.

Here is the query running local to remote:

es2csv -u http://x.x.x.x:9200 -o down.csv -i kaallc -q @'downsample.query' -r
Found 476521 results
Run query [  ] [5601/476521] [  1%] [0:05:46] [ETA: 8:05:11] [ 16.2 docs/s]

Here is the query running directly on the remote Linux instance:

es2csv -u http://x.x.x.x.:9200 -o down.csv -i kaallc -q @'downsample.query' -r
Found 476521 results
Run query [#   ] [169001/476521] [ 35%] [0:02:48] [ETA: 0:05:07] [1000.9 docs/s]

Is there any possible network tuning or other actions I can take to affect the performance?

Thank you.

issue_title: remote DNS resolve has not work with websocket
issue_body:
Hi
the library will call _create_direct_connection() when trying to resolve the url, and a local resolved IP address will be passed to the socks5 connector, so the socks5 will ignore the `rdns` param because the host for the socks5 is already an ip address.


the aiosocksy doesn't have this bug.

issue_title: OrderedDict snapshots match different OrderedDict
issue_body:
A snapshot of an OrderedDict loses its ordering, which makes it match any other dicts with the same items in a different order:

```python
from collections import OrderedDict

def test_ordered_dict(snapshot):
    # create a snapshot named 'ordered_dict'
    expect = OrderedDict([('one', 1), ('two', 2), ('three', 3)])
    snapshot.assert_match(expect, name='ordered_dict')

    # test that named snapshot against the same items in a different order
    # (this simulates a later run where the actual result has changed)
    wrong = OrderedDict([('three', 3), ('two', 2), ('one', 1)])
    snapshot.assert_match(wrong, name='ordered_dict')  # should fail, but doesn't!

    assert expect == wrong  # will fail (correctly), and pytest will produce a diff
```

The problem is that snapshottest casts all dict subclasses to SortedDicts, in both assert_match (via PrettyDiff) and when writing the snapshot file (via Formatter). That loses OrderedDict's ordering.

I'm not really sure how best to fix this...

* Special-casing OrderedDict would solve *this* issue, but other dict subclasses probably have related problems. (E.g., CaseInsensitiveDict is common for code dealing with HTTP headers. The current assert_match would force those case-*sensitive*, causing false failures. And so on... there are a lot of clever dict subclasses out there.)

* Removing PrettyDiff's cast to SortedDict would partly solve the problem, but lead to less-useful diffs for ordinary dicts. (Which is why SortedDict was added in the first place.)

* Maybe PrettyDiff should delay all casting and prettification until get_diff time, so that PrettyDiff.\_\_eq\_\_ always compares the original objects? And Formatter.format_dict should just write the object's \_\_repr\_\_?

* Maybe assert_match shouldn't use PrettyDiff for dicts? The standard equal assertions in both [pytest](https://docs.pytest.org/en/latest/assert.html#making-use-of-context-sensitive-comparisons) and [unittest](https://docs.python.org/3/library/unittest.html#type-specific-methods) already try to provide helpful dict diffs. (So PrettyDiff is actually kind of undermining their built-in failure reporting. Maybe there should be an option to just skip PrettyDiff altogether?)

issue_title: '/' and '\x00' allowed in dict key names and the field names of structured numpy.ndarrays
issue_body:
Null characters causes the HDF5 library to stop reading the string name right there since it uses null terminated strings. So writing to ``'abc\x00defg'`` is the same as writing to ``'abc'``). Furthermore, the ``'/'`` character is used the Group separation character which will cause nested groups to be made.

This is a problem when these values are in ``dict`` keys. They are also a problem when they are in the field names of structured numpy.ndarrays except when  the ``structured_numpy_ndarray_as_struct`` option is ``False``. In the latter case, null characters are still a problem.

issue_title: Document GZIP_CONTENT_TYPES
issue_body:
The `AWS_IS_GZIPPED` and `GZIP_CONTENT_TYPES` settings are not documented in https://django-storages.readthedocs.io/en/latest/backends/amazon-S3.html, which led me to believe they were deprecated.

It's worth mentioning as well that the main page of the docs (https://django-storages.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) has a prominent "settings" link which just links to Azure-specific settings.

issue_title: Use select_for_update for updating task and worker states
issue_body:
Currently we're using Django's automatic transaction management when we call `get_or_create` or `update_or_create`. We should in addition use [`select_for_update`](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.11/ref/models/querysets/#select-for-update) to make sure that the data we're writing to the TaskState and WorkerState models work as intended.

This is especially true if the Camera is deployed in a way that would have multiple instances run side by side for scalability reasons.

issue_title: Rendering may fail if autoescape is not enabled in user-provided jinja environment
issue_body:
To render XML, you need a jinja environment with autoescape that evaluates to True, otherwise _render_xml with fail with an expat error if you feed it strings like "m&m's":
ExpatError: ExpatError "not well-formed (invalid token)" at line 1, column 3210

You currently force autoescape when no environment is passed, which is good.
However, you don't force autoescape when the user provides its own environment.

Now, the problem is that the environment passed by the user may be shared, to produce output for templates of multiple types. For that, the user can provide (since jinja 2.4) in the Environment() constructor an autoescape a function that will return a boolean to tell if escaping is needed, based on the template filename extension. This means you can't just blindly force the autoescape to 'True', as that would break the environment for other (non-XML, non-HTML) template types.

I think the best way to fix this is:
in Renderer.render, check the self.environment.autoescape value. If the value is True, or if it's a function that evaluates to True, all is fine. If it's False, or a function that evaluates to False, then raise an error with sensible message

issue_title: Remove make_result from Query and View
issue_body:
The `make_result` method in View and Query classes is redundant and confusing.  It should be removed.    Each class has a `result` attribute on it that essentially performs the same functionality.

- database module
  - change `get_view_results` to either use the `result` attribute on View or simply standup a new Result object passing it **kwargs.
  - change `get_query_results` to either use the `result` attribute on Query or simply standup a new QueryResult object passing it **kwargs.
- result module
  - Move parameter docs from `make_result` methods to Result/QueryResult class doctoring.
  - Remove references to `make_result` in comments.
- view module
  - Remove `make_result`.
  - Refactor `custom_result` to use either the `result` attribute on View or simply standup a new Result object passing it **kwargs.
  - Add `result` attribute to the class docstring.
- query module
  - Remove `make_result`.
  - Refactor `custom_result` to use either the `result` attribute on Query or simply standup a new QueryResult object passing it **kwargs.
    - Add `result` attribute to the class docstring.
- Tests
  - Ensure current tests still work.
  - Remove `make_result` tests.
  - Add additional tests to test the `result` attribute on both Query and View.

issue_title: get memory error when running a model exported from gluon model zoo
issue_body:
I tried to export resnet from the Gluon model zoo and run it in the Symbol executor, but got a serious memory error. The memory error is also non-deterministic. Here is the minimal code. I need to run it multiple times to reproduce the error.

```python
import numpy as np
import mxnet as mx
import copy
import random
import itertools
from numpy.testing import assert_allclose, assert_array_equal
from mxnet.test_utils import *
from mxnet.gluon.model_zoo.vision import get_model

def test_make_subgraph():
    def create_weights(shapes, names):
        nd_dict = {}
        assert len(shapes) == len(names)
        for i in range(len(shapes)):
            nd_dict[names[i]] = mx.nd.array(np.ones(shapes[i]), ctx=default_context())
        return nd_dict

    def make_subgraph4(stype):
        model = get_model('resnet18_v1')
        model.hybridize()
        model.initialize()
        shape = (1, 3, 32, 32)
        data = mx.nd.random.normal(shape=shape)
        out = model(data)
        model.export('resnet18')
        orig = mx.sym.load('resnet18-symbol.json')

        arg_shapes, out_shapes, aux_shapes = orig.infer_shape(data=shape)
        weight_shapes = arg_shapes[1:]
        weight_names = orig.list_arguments()[1:]
        weight_dict = create_weights(weight_shapes, weight_names)
        aux_dict = create_weights(aux_shapes, orig.list_auxiliary_states())

        arr = mx.nd.random.uniform(-1, 1, shape=shape, ctx=default_context()).tostype(stype)
        arg_dict = weight_dict
        arg_dict['data'] = arr
        return (orig, arg_dict, aux_dict)

    orig, inputs, aux_states = make_subgraph4('default')
    all_inputs = copy.deepcopy(inputs)
    all_inputs.update(aux_states)
    args_grad = {key : mx.nd.empty(shape=all_inputs[key].shape) for key in all_inputs.keys()}
    e1 = orig.bind(ctx=default_context(), args=all_inputs, args_grad=args_grad,
                   aux_states=all_inputs)
    args_grad = {key : mx.nd.empty(shape=all_inputs[key].shape) for key in all_inputs.keys()}
    e1.forward()
    mx.nd.waitall()

    out_grads = [mx.nd.random.uniform(-1, 1, shape=out.shape, ctx=default_context())
                 for out in e1.outputs]
    e1.backward(out_grads)
    mx.nd.waitall()

test_make_subgraph()
```

issue_title: document exceptions that PGPKey.decrypt can raise
issue_body:
It seems I forgot to document the exception(s) that PGPKey.decrypt can raise, and what conditions cause them to be raised. They are:


- if the message was not encrypted by this key, `PGPError` is raised
- if the symmetric key was encrypted using an asymmetric algorithm that PGPy does not (yet) support, `NotImplementedError` is raised
- if the decrypted symmetric key checksum is wrong, or if message decryption fails, `PGPDecryptionError` is rasised

issue_title: Should StreamingBody proxy underlying stream attributes?
issue_body:
When wrapping a raw stream, which is exactly what `botocore.response.StreamingBody` does, it would be nice to have more access to the raw stream. 

My specific use case is calling `stream.seek(0)`. 

Basically, I don't think `StreamingBody` fulfills the ABC of `io.IOBase` in the standard library (https://docs.python.org/3.4/library/io.html#io.IOBase) and it should fully implement it. The easiest way to do that is to proxy attributes down to the raw stream, like how werkzeug does it for its `FileStorage` class (http://werkzeug.pocoo.org/docs/0.11/datastructures/#werkzeug.datastructures.FileStorage).

issue_title: Remove large files in git history
What does async mean for nexmo-python?
issue_body:
Async frameworks use generators for HTTP requests, so that these potentially blocking calls can be `await`ed. As these frameworks become more popular (and they seem to be a major motivator for moving to Python 3) it will be important to support them.

- Do we need to do anything? Is it possible to just use blocking calls in an async way? (ie. can we make this the user's problem)
- Is there a low-effort solution, like adding a decorator to each function, that automatically makes it async if it detects an async environment? Can we just stick a thin wrapper over the top?
- Do we need a separate 'async-native' solution? The Python IO landscape seems to be changing, with lots of new libraries like httpio and sanic being produced for async requirements.

issue_title: Better names for `Current Frame Contains`, `Focus` and `Simulate`
issue_body:
Keywords listed on the summary are all somewhat badly named.

- `Current Frame Contains` should be renamed to `Current Frame Should Contain` to follow Robot's general naming convention.
- `Focus` and `Simulate` are not explicit enough. Better names are `Set Focus To Element` and `Simulate Event`.

We can add these new names in SL 3.0, but actually getting them changed isn't too urgent so we don't need to loudly deprecate them yet. We can do official deprecation in SL 3.1 and the remove them in SL 3.2 or later.

issue_title: Add support for Robot's programmatic logging APIs
issue_body:
As far as I can tell, the way how logging works in combination with the PythonRemoteServer depends very much on when the corresponding logging StreamHandler is first created.
If it is created before _intercept_std_streams() is called, then the logging output will be sent to stderr of the server. If it is created after _intercept_std_streams(), then the output is sent as `output` to the calling client.

I fixed this for my use case with this change to _intercept_std_streams(), which makes sure that I receive all log messages logged during run_keyword() on the robotframework client:

``` python
    def _intercept_std_streams(self):
        sys.stdout = StringIO()
        sys.stderr = StringIO()
        # reset the logging handler so that it is connected to the right stderr stream
        import logging
        logging.getLogger().handlers = []
        ch = logging.StreamHandler()
        logging.getLogger().addHandler(ch)
```

NOTE: I mostly opened this issue so that it is documented in case other people run into similiar problems. I'm not sure if this is something that can/should be handled by the default implementation of PythonRemoteServer, since you would have to consider a myriad of possible python logging configurations. Feel free to close.

issue_title: Add an intuitive way to use Pipenv without a home directory to facilitate use in deployment scenarios
issue_body:
## The problem
I attempted to automate deployment of some Python code that uses Pipenv to isolate itself and lock its dependencies. The automation is in the form of a Chef cookbook. I ran into frustration while executing normal commands such as `pipenv install` due to the fact that Pipenv makes liberal use of the current value of `HOME` and assumes that whatever is in `HOME` is readable and writeable by the executing user. However, my Chef cookbook executes commands as an unprivileged user, in a very clean environment where typical environment variables such as `HOME` are not set (unless hacks are used). The usage of an unprivileged user and clean environment are engineering standards set by the organization I work for.

## The temporary workaround
I was able to get the deployment process to work in a way that avoids touching the home directory, by using environment variables such as `PIPENV_VENV_IN_PROJECT`, `PIPENV_CACHE_DIR`, and `PIP_CACHE_DIR`. It could also conceivably work to spoof the `HOME` variable with some non-home directory.

## The solution I'd like
Assumptions about the current user's home directory and environment variables should be documented explicitly, with the mindset that Pipenv is a tool that will be used in automation frameworks where these things are not guaranteed.

It would be ideal to avoid environment variable trickery and create a clean, streamlined deployment process. Pipenv could allow an option or "mode" that abandons all assumptions about the current user's home directory. This could be in the form of a command-line flag, such as `--no-home-directory` (just for example).

## Alternatives I've considered
An alternative solution is to change the way that we approach deployments at my organization, or to make a special case for this application. I have spoken with other people in my organization about setting the home directory to be readable and writeable for this particular cookbook, to avoid kludges and workarounds.

issue_title: The default function in HashingPartitioner is wrong
issue_body:
https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/2cb39da37356d3a73554325a663915698d4dbb97/pykafka/partitioners.py#L53-L61

Bad Python form. If you want anything resembling consistency, `hash` should never be used. There's no guarantee that it's going to produce consistent results between architectures and interpreter restarts. Instead, it should be using sha1 or, even better, mmh3, as the default.

issue_title: Error/gain API
issue_body:
In the discussion of https://github.com/astropy/photutils/pull/160, @cdeil suggests removing the `gain` parameter.  In the photometry routines, `gain` is currently used with a background-only `error` to compute the total `error`, including the Poisson noise of the sources:

`error_tot = np.sqrt(error_b**2 + (data / gain))`

where `error_b` is the "background" error (really all sources of error except source Poisson error) and `gain` (not CCD gain) is a conversion factor when multiplied by the data puts the data in count units (e.g. electrons or photons) where Poisson statistics can be applied.

A function could be provided to compute `error_tot` from `error_b` and `gain`.  We could then remove the `gain` parameter and require the photometry routines to accept only the total `error`.

However, the detection routines (e.g. `detect_sources`) require an `error` input that is *only* the "background" error.  This is because source detections are determined by a significance level above the background noise.  Without a `gain` parameter, one would need to carry around both the background-only error array (e.g., `background_rms`) for the detection routines and a total `error` array for the photometry routines.  In this case, I also think the error input parameters for detection and photometry routines should have different names to limit potential confusion.

Any thoughts about which is preferable?

scikit-image claims to require numpy >= 1.7.2. However, for scikit-image 0.12.3 the manylinux1 wheels were built against a newer numpy. issue_title: review the hand-crafted ASM code in the codebase
issue_body:
From: https://issues.numenta.org/browse/NPC-321

Some files in our codebase include hand-tuned assembly code (that mostly utilizes SSE instructions) : 

```
$ grep -R 'mov ' | egrep -v '.git|Makefile.in|external'
nta/algorithms/svm.hpp:                   mov eax, f
nta/algorithms/svm.hpp:                   mov d, edx
nta/algorithms/svm_t.hpp:        mov     esi, x
nta/algorithms/svm_t.hpp:        mov     edi, y
nta/math/array_algo.hpp:      mov eax, f
nta/math/array_algo.hpp:        mov c, ecx
nta/math/array_algo.hpp:        mov d, edx
```

These files provide speed optimizations (super good!!), but have several disadvantages: 
- hard to understand - very few people can review,port,update them
- mostly only for 32bit Mac - uncommon (64bits)
- can cause tricky platform-specific bugs (see https://github.com/numenta/nupic/pull/181 for NTA_ASM on/off flag)

Steps I'd like to propose: 
1. Can we use an optimized library's functions instead? ( https://issues.numenta.org/browse/NPC-320 )
2. port the ASM to 64bits, linux too
3. moaaahr optimizationz!! :)

issue_title: unreleased versions are not installed when setup has 1.4.3 as stable
issue_body: The `setup.py`'s setup() method indicates the stable version to be installed. Because the unreleased branch of qds-sdk has `1.4.3` as the stable version, downstream tools that want the unreleased versions by specifying a github dependency pointed to the unreleased branch will fail to install. This is because setuptools finds `1.4.3` to be stable over `unreleased` and always override with what is found in PyPi. In order to  install `unreleased` versions the "stable" version in the unreleased branch should be made `unreleased`

issue_title: Decode MetaBlock Multithreading
issue_body:
More an idea as an issue.
Every MetaBlock could decode standalone in a thread.

Now an issue.
The blocksize is stored in meta-block header. (see spec. 9.2.) But only the final uncompressed size of block. The compressed size length isn't stored. That is a problem. Because the next thread will not know the position to start parallel the decoding of next block.

Solution:
Include a store information about compression block size.

issue_title: Refactor portfolio level returns in analyze_sim_run notebook
issue_body:
Currently we compute portfolio level returns in the analyze_sim_run notebook using a weighted average of the returns to each algo in the portfolio.

It would be more proper to compute portfolio level returns from the aggregated portfolio state. Gus did take a stab at doing this, but we then discovered his first implementation had a bug in that it did not account for capital being rebalanced across algos monthly and erroneously treated that capital being added or removed as PNL.

The fix was to roll back to a weighted average of returns streams, but we'd prefer to have the aggregated portfolio state method properly handle capital in/out flows in the future. 

cc @gusgordon @twiecki

issue_title: SConstruct on darwin insists on Xcode and ignores command line tools
issue_body:
I am having trouble building Galsim on my Mac because I do not have Xcode installed but I am using the Apple command line tools. The problem is that an attempt is made to determine the Xcode version by invoking `xcodebuild` and this fails. Can the Xcode version determination be deferred until it might be needed? If TMV is not linking against BLAS then the whole test is irrelevant. The code is currently:
```python
p = subprocess.Popen(['xcodebuild','-version'], stdout=subprocess.PIPE)
xcode_version = p.stdout.readlines()[0].split()[1]
print 'XCode version is',xcode_version
if (platform.mac_ver()[0] >= '10.7' and #xcode_version < '5.1' and
    '-latlas' not in tmv_link and ('-lblas' in tmv_link or '-lcblas' in tmv_link)):
    print 'WARNING: The Apple BLAS library has been found not to be thread safe on'
    print '         Mac OS versions 10.7+, even across multiple processes (i.e. not'
    print '         just multiple threads in the same process.)  The symptom is that'
    print '         `scons tests` may hang when running nosetests using multiple'
    print '         processes.'
    if xcode_version < '5.1':
        print '         This seems to have been partially fixed with XCode 5.1, so we'
        print '         recommend upgrading to the latest XCode version.  However, even'
        print '         with 5.1, some systems still seem to have problems.'
        env['BAD_BLAS'] = True
    else:
        print '         This seems to have been partially fixed with XCode 5.1, so there'
        print '         is a good chance you will not have any problems.  But there are'
        print '         still occasional systems that fail when using multithreading with'
        print '         programs or modules that link to the BLAS library (such as GalSim).'
        print '         If you do have problems, the solution is to recompile TMV with'
        print '         the SCons option "WITH_BLAS=false".'
```
but changing it to something like:
```python
if (platform.mac_ver()[0] >= '10.7' and
    '-latlas' not in tmv_link and ('-lblas' in tmv_link or '-lcblas' in tmv_link)):
    p = subprocess.Popen(['xcodebuild','-version'], stdout=subprocess.PIPE)
    xcode_version = p.stdout.readlines()[0].split()[1]
    print 'XCode version is',xcode_version
   if xcode_version < '5.1':
```
would at least defer the breakage to a situation where BLAS is involved. I think the only real fix for this is to treat the clang version as a proxy for Xcode version.

issue_title: duplicate point removal not applicable to all file types
issue_body:
Duplicate point removal is only applied to HDF5 files. The reason for this is that by construction this format has duplicate points between adjoining sections. However, it would make sense to have a uniform treatment of data, where duplicate points can be removed *optionally* regardless of data format.

See related issue #177.

issue_title: Security for API
issue_body:
Currently the API is completely unsecured.  This limits us adding new features to the API which could actually destroy or reveal data, and isn't a great idea in general.  As such, I'd like two things for the API:

* simple user/password auth
* SSL support

There's two ways we could approach this: one would be to add these things using various snippets to the existing API.py (for example: https://gist.github.com/fxsjy/5465353).  The second way would be to offer sample configurations for an nginx proxy for the API.

Thoughts?

issue_title: Migrate to PyPi's new release system
issue_body:
We are currently uploading new library versions to the legacy pypi.python.org infrastructure using `python setup.py upload`. This is no more recommended as the new infrastructure will [eventually become the only one](https://packaging.python.org/guides/migrating-to-pypi-org/), and as this method of uploading packages is [insecure and now **strongly discouraged**](https://packaging.python.org/tutorials/distributing-packages/#uploading-your-project-to-pypi).

I propose that we update the release procedure to target the new infrastructure and to use the recommended `twine` method, [as done in the django repository](https://github.com/algolia/algoliasearch-django/pull/224/files).

issue_title: chalice deployment package missing libraries
issue_body:
When my Bitbucket pipeline runs `chalice deploy --stage dev`, the deployment works just fine. When I run `chalice deploy --stage dev` from my command line, the package deployed to AWS is missing most libraries... so I get errors like `Unable to import module 'app': No module named 'phpserialize'` when the Lambda is executed.

I've verified that the deployment packages differ by running deployments, downloading the deployment packages from AWS (using the Lambda console), and diff'ing the contents of the .zip files.

Clearly there's some difference between my personal environment and the Bitbucket pipeline environment that causes a difference in how the deployment .zip is built, but I can't figure it out. Here's what I've tried:
- Changing the commands to/from `chalice deploy` && `chalice deploy --stage dev`. No affect.
- Rebuilding my vendor dependencies: In the past I've been able to 'fix' deployments missing modules by manually rebuilding the wheels in the `vendor` directory, but this hasn't worked in the past few days: ```pip download x && pip wheel x-y.y.y.tar.gz && rm *.gz```
- python/pip versions: Bitbucket was using python 3.6.3 & pip 9.0.1 and my personal machine was python 3.6.5 & pip 9.0.3. I changed my versions 3.6.3/9.0.1 and my deployment packages were still missing libraries. I changed the Bitbucket pipeline to 3.6.5/10.0.1 and the deployment was still perfect.

issue_title: Cannot run chalice deploy with '--stage' parameter when using @app.on_s3_event
issue_body:
This one is fairly obvious. 

Using chalice for s3 event triggers means you must have a pre-existing s3 bucket. When deploying with the stage parameter this creates you a new role(if enabled) and lambda but still the s3 bucket must pre-exist.

Because of this, the behavior actions performed on the bucket remains the same as a normal `chalice deploy`. **Chalice will put an event on the s3 bucket to point to the new lambda.**

So if I run `chalice deploy` and then `chalice deploy --stage test` I get this:
```
(env) GC02RK59EG8WPE:photo_analytics trentonmcfarlane$ chalice deploy --stage test
Creating deployment package.
Updating lambda function: photo_analytics-test-handler
Configuring S3 events in bucket photo-bucket-trent to function photo_analytics-test-handler
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/chalice/deploy/deployer.py", line 330, in deploy
    return self._deploy(config, chalice_stage_name)
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/chalice/deploy/deployer.py", line 343, in _deploy
    self._executor.execute(plan)
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/chalice/deploy/executor.py", line 31, in execute
    self._default_handler)(instruction)
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/chalice/deploy/executor.py", line 43, in _do_apicall
    result = method(**final_kwargs)
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/chalice/awsclient.py", line 767, in connect_s3_bucket_to_lambda
    NotificationConfiguration=existing_config,
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/botocore/client.py", line 314, in _api_call
    return self._make_api_call(operation_name, kwargs)
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/botocore/client.py", line 612, in _make_api_call
    raise error_class(parsed_response, operation_name)
botocore.exceptions.ClientError: An error occurred (InvalidArgument) when calling the PutBucketNotificationConfiguration operation: Configuration is ambiguously defined. Cannot have overlapping suffixes in two rules if the prefixes are overlapping for the same event type.

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/chalice/cli/__init__.py", line 447, in main
    return cli(obj={})
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/click/core.py", line 722, in __call__
    return self.main(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/click/core.py", line 697, in main
    rv = self.invoke(ctx)
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/click/core.py", line 1066, in invoke
    return _process_result(sub_ctx.command.invoke(sub_ctx))
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/click/core.py", line 895, in invoke
    return ctx.invoke(self.callback, **ctx.params)
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/click/core.py", line 535, in invoke
    return callback(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/click/decorators.py", line 17, in new_func
    return f(get_current_context(), *args, **kwargs)
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/chalice/cli/__init__.py", line 183, in deploy
    deployed_values = d.deploy(config, chalice_stage_name=stage)
  File "/Users/trentonmcfarlane/personal/photo_analytics/env/lib/python3.6/site-packages/chalice/deploy/deployer.py", line 332, in deploy
    raise ChaliceDeploymentError(e)
chalice.deploy.deployer.ChaliceDeploymentError: ERROR - While deploying your chalice application, received the following error:

 An error occurred (InvalidArgument) when calling the
 PutBucketNotificationConfiguration operation: Configuration is ambiguously
 defined. Cannot have overlapping suffixes in two rules if the prefixes are
 overlapping for the same event type.
```

The major takeaway:
 PutBucketNotificationConfiguration operation: Configuration is ambiguously
 defined. Cannot have overlapping suffixes in two rules if the prefixes are
 overlapping for the same event type.


Regardless, chalice doesn't deploy s3 buckets and therefore you would be sharing a bucket across your stages, which is not good practice.


Solutions:

1. Deploy s3 buckets (big feature, not sure this is possible)
2. Manually deploy s3 buckets for each stage and define them in the config.json


The latter isn't a great option but it at least works around the problem.

issue_title: Running dev_appserver on remote machine behind a proxy
issue_body:
I have managed to put myself into a very special circumstance (or maybe OS vendors have), where I can't find a stable project development solution, which I would enjoy; I have been using Chromebook for development with Cloud 9 and alike services. I like the paradigm of developing on remote servers, because you don't have to deal with the bugs and system malfunctions, which tend to plague the virtualization tools on Windows and OS X (people say they work, but I think they haven't tried cloud development paradigm). I have been very happy developer for past three years... until I switched jobs and have to use HTTP development tools like the admin panel of AppEngine.

Some tools bend to my will more easily than others. With AppEngine the problem is, that you are checking the Host headers for security reasons and only allow localhost, I tried overriding that with a header rewrite, but you use relative URIs only, which read the Host header; so either I can not access datastore features or the links do not work, which makes the panel quite useless.

Some other tools, which do bend to my will more easily have solved this issue by allowing you to define root_path variable; so in the admin panel software you will use absolute paths, which can be defaulted to the Host and overridden if such variable is present.

I believe I could also just buy a domain, but some users might not want to do that.

Of course there might already be a solution, but I haven't been able to discover it, so feel free to point me towards right direction.

issue_title: Remove the need to specify scala tools in BUILD.tools
issue_body:
All (?) other tools now have default classspaths ([via](https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/2778/)), but because the scala tools do not have a reasonable global default, they currently need to be manually set in BUILD.tools.

https://rbcommons.com/s/twitter/r/2891/ added basic support for scala platforms. This ticket should deal with improving the scala platform support to allow for configuring _only_ the scala platform version (ie `2.11`), and having the compiler/library/etc use default values for those platforms (ie `2.11.7` being the most recent release of the `2.11` platform).

After this ticket, it should be possible to build Java code in a brand new pants install without configuring scala tools.

issue_title: Android examples cannot all be compiled in one run
issue_body:
Before isolated compile, the `unpacked_libraries` and `dx_compile` tasks operated over AndroidLibrary targets. But now they need to be refactored to work over AndroidBinary targets instead. 

Those tasks unpack .aar archives and create AndroidLibrary targets from their contents. This included adding a JarLibrary dependency (from the classes.jar inside the aar archives) to every dependent AndroidLibrary. But those classes.jars are now needed on the classpath of the actual AndroidBinary targets in order to compile. This previously just worked under global_compile strategy, but in a correctness sense those jars should be dependencies of the AndroidBinaries.

I have hacked the tests to go green in an attempt to move android backend to contrib. But if a pants run globs multiple android targets, the graph gets constructed incorrectly and dependencies are missing.

To repro: pants bundle examples/src/android::
To fix: have unpacked_libraries operate on AndroidBinary targets. That change is not so bad but it will also have to be reflected in DxCompile, which filters each unpacked_library based on the include/exclude pattern of the Binary's AndroidLibrary targets. So a major rethink is possibly in order.

issue_title: `--cache-ignore` behavior isn't the same as `invalidate` before
issue_body:
With the removal of `invalidate` (https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/commit/2d4e61753d3896ee969ba239cb0a66c36d781f84#diff-0295a620e939c7397353c955d452ae25), there is no good way to force a task to recheck for cache anymore after a successfully run because everything is validated. This is important because sometimes we need to make sure a task will hit the cache with invalidation, whereas `clean-all` will wipe out a lot of other info under `.pants.d/` along with pants server content.

I'd like to propose to add it back. @stuhood @jsirois @benjyw, thoughts?

issue_title: Some Windows test builds are failing to install lxml

We're getting variations of [this error message] (https://ci.appveyor.com/project/fgregg/usaddress/build/1.0.50/job/ovarsc9aqjsgxbfw#L129) in builds for Python 3.4: 

```

```

The error seems to happen when Appveyor has to build lxml from source ([sample here, Python 3.4 on x64](https://ci.appveyor.com/project/fgregg/usaddress/build/1.0.50/job/a5a1f3atkm1iodt4)). issue_title: API_V1_ROOT missing backslash causing requests to fail
issue_body:
API_V1_ROOT is `"api/v1"` when it looks like it should be `"/api/v1"` or requests to the API fail because `otx.alienvault.comapi` is not a valid hostname for HTTP requests. I'm on Python 2.7.11 just fyi.

- **Line:** https://github.com/AlienVault-Labs/OTX-Python-SDK/blob/master/OTXv2.py#L17

Simply prepending a backslash to the API_V1_ROOT variable fixed this for me when I reinstalled the latest master branch locally. Added the backslash to the line above, ran `pip install . --upgrade`, and it worked as expected.

**API call & Traceback:**
```
>>> mirai_iocs = otx.get_pulse_indicators('58e39804b789b25b15083fc6', limit=219)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<input>", line 1, in <module>
    mirai_iocs = otx.get_pulse_indicators('58e39804b789b25b15083fc6', limit=219)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/OTXv2.py", line 404, in get_pulse_indicators
    return self.walkapi(self.create_url(PULSE_DETAILS + str(pulse_id) + "/indicators", limit=limit))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/OTXv2.py", line 274, in walkapi
    return list(self.walkapi_iter(url, max_page=max_page))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/OTXv2.py", line 265, in walkapi_iter
    data = self.get(next_page_url)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/OTXv2.py", line 112, in get
    proxies=self.proxies,
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/requests/sessions.py", line 501, in get
    return self.request('GET', url, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/requests/sessions.py", line 488, in request
    resp = self.send(prep, **send_kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/requests/sessions.py", line 609, in send
    r = adapter.send(request, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/requests/adapters.py", line 487, in send
    raise ConnectionError(e, request=request)
ConnectionError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='otx.alienvault.comapi', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /v1/pulses/58e39804b789b25b15083fc6/indicators?limit=219 (Caused by NewConnectionError('<requests.packages.urllib3.connection.VerifiedHTTPSConnection object at 0x10c0d5110>: Failed to establish a new connection: [Errno 8] nodename nor servname provided
, or not known',))
```

As shown above the ConnectionError is failing due to `HTTPSConnectionPool(host='otx.alienvault.comapi', port=443)` where the host variable is not a valid hostname.

**After Patch:**
```
>>> from OTXv2 import OTXv2
>>> otx = OTXv2(key)
>>> mirai_iocs = otx.get_pulse_indicators('58e39804b789b25b15083fc6', limit=100)
```

issue_title: pulp silently ignores 'double constraints'
issue_body:
In Python, it is possible to test for a variable to be within a range (ie. 1 <= a <= 5). Hence, this syntax is also legal in pulp constraints - however the first part is silently ignored.

I recommend one of three resolutions:
a) allow the constraint to directly work as entered
b) automatically split the constraint set in two sets - one for the first part and one for the second
c) keep the current behavior but at least issue a warning

Test case:
import pulp
a = pulp.LpVariable('a', lowBound=0, cat=pulp.LpContinuous)
milp = pulp.LpProblem("TC", pulp.LpMinimize)
milp += a
milp += 1 <= a <= 5
print(milp)

Expected behavior:
a >= 1 and hence the optimal solution is a = 1 OR a warning is issued that "1 <=" will be ignored

Actual behavior:
First part of constraint `milp += 1 <= a <= 5` (1 <= a) is silently ignored.

issue_title: All of the Canadian clients return datetime data type for 'timestamp'
issue_body:
Each of the ISO / balancing authority clients returns a `list` of `dict` objects with key / values described at: https://pyiso.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html#clients However, there's no explicit mention of what data type the value for the `timestamp` key should be. In all of the Canadian clients, a timezone-aware UTC `datetime` value is returned. After discussion with WattTimer folks, returning `pandas.Timestamp` in UTC is the correct data type (for the time being). 

Update all returned values for 'timestamp' dictionary entries to `pandas.Timestamp` in the Canadian clients.

issue_title: Generic error handlers are exiting with status 0, not status 1
issue_body:
This was something found in #32 and fixed there.
However, in case that work is cancelled, delayed, or significantly altered, we need to be sure to fix this issue.

When the custom excepthook was replaced with a high-level dispatch on exception types, I did not correctly add explicit `exit(1)` calls as I should have.

issue_title: Failed to import volume from Unity
issue_body: When importing volume from Unity, lun.pool.name will be queried by cinder driver to match volume host, but nested_properties are specified for lun and pool.name is not in nested properties fields, so lun.pool.name return None and volume export failed.

issue_title: Use big5-2003 instead of big5
issue_body:
I have met a use case that chardet detected the file as big5, but it can't be converted by `iconv` totally, after some searching I found it's big5-2003 with some characters that aren't in big5. See attachment.

Since big5-2003 is compatible with big5 and with more characters, I guess it's better to use big5-2003 instead of big5, and likewise the GB18030 and GB2312


[1.txt](https://github.com/chardet/chardet/files/1886931/1.txt)

issue_title: Parallelize the calculation of p-values
Serializable.get_sedes() breaks inheritance when child defines different fields
issue_body:
That method [caches the sedes in the _sedes class variable](https://github.com/ethereum/pyrlp/blob/develop/rlp/sedes/lists.py#L226), so if you call get_sedes() on a parent class, any childs of that class on which get_sedes() has not been called yet will end up with the parent's sedes no matter what is defined in their fields attribute. 

```
class CustomSerializable(rlp.Serializable):
    pass

class A(rlp.Serializable):
    fields = [('foo', rlp.Serializable)]

class B(A):
    fields = [('foo', CustomSerializable)]

print(A.get_sedes())
print(B.get_sedes())

[<class 'rlp.sedes.lists.Serializable'>]
[<class 'rlp.sedes.lists.Serializable'>]
```

issue_title: Try to return headers during fast sync
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

During fast sync we respond to all `GetBlockHeader` messages with an empty array of headers.  We however, *may* have the requested headers in our database.


### How can it be fixed

We should *try* to return the headers that were requested.

issue_title: Overwritten transactions are not removed
issue_body:
* Version: 0.1.0b15
* Python: 3.5
* OS: all

### What was wrong?

When submitting a transaction that overwrites another transaction, the original transaction is not removed and calls to `getTransaction()` still return the old transaction instead of null.

In geth, the transaction would be removed and returned as `None` from `getTransaction()`.

Example:

```
tester = EthereumTester(auto_mine_transactions=False)

web3 = Web3(EthereumTesterProvider(tester))

original_tx = web3.eth.sendTransaction({
    'from': '...',
    'to': '...',
    'value': 1,
    'gas': 23500,
    'gasPrice': 10,
    'nonce': 0
})

print(web3.eth.getTransaction(original_tx))

replacement_tx = web3.eth.sendTransaction({
    'from': '...',
    'to': '...',
    'value': 2,
    'gas': 23500,
    'gasPrice': 20,
    'nonce': 0
})

print(web3.eth.getTransaction(replacement_tx))
print(web3.eth.getTransaction(original_tx))  # Should be null
```

### How can it be fixed?

Unsure whether this is a backend problem or not but i imagine there needs to be some tx pool managing functionality added.

issue_title: conda locking rudimentary and fragile
issue_body:
The conda lock is currently implemented using a two-step `glob()` / `open()` sequence. This has several issues:
* there is an inherent race condition: two processes could satisfy the `glob()` condition and take the lock at the same time
* the lock isn't cleaned up when a process terminates abruptly (which can happen when e.g. a CI job gets killed)

There are various ways to do robust file-based locking. Cross-platform Python libraries exist for this, e.g. [locket](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/locket/) or [py-filelock](https://filelock.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).

issue_title: Creating empty environment can trick user
issue_body:
When creating an empty environment, as suggested by the documentation, a real gotcha is that installing by pip will install into the root environment. I suggest that the documentation be updated to warn the user. 

To clarify - when a user creates a new **empty** environment, pip is not installed into this environment, but is still linked from root. I can foresee new users installing conda, creating a new environment, and then installing their favourite package via pip, and consequently running into issues when the package cannot be found.

Previously, this was not an issue, since environments were created by `conda create --name myenv PACKAGE`, which wasn't very intuitive, but prevented an empty environment from being created.
